what_it_does: This file presents an architectural thesis stating that the company is a single composition substrate of addressed nodes, typed edges, and composed work, resolved through one resolver, and it derives a build strategy of connecting existing nearby pieces rather than greenfield invention.
observations: Declares a one-machine architecture where graphs, chains, flows, cascades, compositions, and the dragnet are all faces of the same substrate.
Defines the primitive as an addressed NODE plus typed EDGES plus composed WORK.
States registries are file-discovered and fail-loud.
Quotes Tim: "a dragnet is one use of graphs, to go with the chains".
Argues most parts already exist and are "one wire apart", so work is connect + extend + finish.
Uses standard noticeboard item YAML front matter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history.
standouts: The one-machine thesis collapses many abstractions into a single substrate.
Explicit reference to runtime/cognition.py:resolve_address as the universal resolver.
File-discovered, fail-loud registries as a core mechanism.
The "one wire apart" framing for build strategy.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item format with YAML front matter and Markdown body.
Uses a part_of link to a parent item.
Records creation and update timestamps and a filed history entry.
Mentions a code path in prose rather than as a formal symbol reference.
concerns: The thesis is asserted without supporting examples or evidence inside the file.
The resolver path is referenced but not verified or linked to an actual symbol.
"One wire apart" and "fail-loud" are used metaphorically without concrete definition here.
Only one parent link exists; no links to the resolver or to the systems it claims are nearby.
notes: A noticeboard thesis arguing the whole company should be treated as one node-edge-work machine and built by wiring existing parts instead of inventing new ones.
questions: What are the exact APIs or schemas for NODE, EDGES, and WORK?
Which specific systems or files are currently "one wire apart"?
What does "fail-loud" look like in practice for file-discovered registries?
How does this thesis relate to the parent item-228f99c7?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose (present the one-machine thesis and its consequence for the build) matches what the file does.
apparent_themes: Multiple surface forms over one substrate
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to establish a shared architectural framing that justifies integrating existing systems and directs builders to connect nearby pieces rather than build new ones.
novelty: It frames all company abstractions as faces of a single underlying machine with a single resolver and file-discovered registries.
contribution: A concise architectural thesis and a build heuristic (connect existing pieces that are one wire apart) that can guide integration decisions.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard item asserting the company is a single node-edge-work composition substrate resolved by runtime/cognition.py:resolve_address, where graphs, chains, flows, cascades, compositions, and the dragnet are surface forms, and the build strategy is to connect existing nearby pieces rather than invent new ones.
intention_for_embedding: To articulate a unifying one-machine architecture and shift the build posture from greenfield invention to connecting existing substrate uses.
